question.text: Under SEBI (Investment Advisers) Regulations, 2013, the certificate of registration granted to an investment adviser is valid:

answer.text: For a period of 5 years from the date of grant
Till it is suspended or cancelled by SEBI
For a period of 3 years, subject to renewal
For a period of 10 years from the date of grant

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per SEBI IA Regulations 2013, the certificate of registration is valid till it is suspended or cancelled by SEBI — there is no fixed expiry period for the registration itself. **The Trap:** Many candidates confuse NISM certification validity (which needs periodic renewal) with IA registration validity. The registration is perpetual unless suspended/cancelled. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series X-A tests the same concept in the regulatory chapter. Series III-A covers intermediary registration validity broadly. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** While the IA registration doesn't expire, the NISM certification does — ensure timely renewal of the certification to maintain compliance.
